trivia_bullets.text: Wrap in Flames is a five-printing card with a 10-year reprint history: Rise of the Eldrazi (2010), Conspiracy (2014), Modern Masters 2015, Battlebond (2018), and Theros Beyond Death (2020). The THB printing features new artwork by Kari Christensen — distinct from the earlier Véronique Meignaud art used in Rise of the Eldrazi and its reprints. The nightmare-horse art on the THB printing specifically anchors the card in Theros's underworld aesthetic, where nightmare horses (horselike creatures from the realm of Erebos) appear across the set. [Scryfall prints search; Scryfall ROE/173 artist field]

Flavor text: "Bright flames quell the darkest nightmares." The line doubles as both literal (fire drives back nightmare horses) and thematic — in Theros mythology the Underworld's dead and nightmare creatures fear light and the living world's warmth. [Scryfall flavor_text: thb/164]

The oracle text — dealing 1 damage to up to three targets and tapping them out of blocking — makes this a combat trick / tempo play that straddles sorcery timing. In limited formats it functions as a multi-creature pseudo-tap or a way to push through damage against a defensive board. EDHREC rank 23,417 reflects minimal Commander penetration; the card's value is primarily in limited and Pauper-legal formats. [Scryfall legalities field; Scryfall edhrec_rank]

The THB version's four-mana cost ({3}{R}) is consistent across all five printings — Wizards has reprinted the card text verbatim across a decade without functional errata. This makes Wrap in Flames one of the stable tempo-common designs in red that Wizards returns to for multi-player / limited reprint sets (appearing in Conspiracy and Battlebond, both multiplayer-focused sets). [Scryfall prints search: oracleid b46efd59]
